title: Tapping to Anchor Lifelong Financial Ease
subtitle: A tapping practice for settling ease as the permanent baseline for your financial life
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 70 of a 12-week money program. The script acknowledges how much has changed across the programme and anchors the new relationship with money (calm, trust, and ease) as a permanent foundation rather than a temporary state. The final tapping practice of Week 10.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 70 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a long period of money work when you want to acknowledge the shift and anchor ease as a permanent new baseline, not a phase that will end.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 70 of a 12-week money program. Day 70 is a completion point — the final day of Week 10, building unshakable money trust. The script acknowledges how much has shifted across the programme and anchors ease, calm, and trust in money as a permanent foundation rather than the result of a temporary good patch.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once lived in money tension, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though scarcity used to feel normal, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I carried that for years, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I see how much has changed.
Side of eye: My relationship with money is calm.
Under eye: Abundance feels natural and steady.
Under nose: I trust money to keep growing.
Chin: I live without financial fear now.
Collarbone: I'm proud of this new foundation.
Under arm: I feel peaceful about every bill and plan.
Top of head: I anchor ease as my permanent state.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I live in lifelong financial ease.
Side of eye: I trust wealth to keep supporting me.
Under eye: I stay relaxed and confident about money.
Under nose: I expect abundance to flow and grow.
Chin: I enjoy creating from a place of security.
Collarbone: I feel grateful for a life of steady overflow.
Under arm: I carry this trust into every future year.
Top of head: I am anchored in calm, lasting prosperity.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 70 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
